title: Packaged documents
description: Packaged markdown reference topics printed from the installed you-agent-factory CLI via you docs.
openingSummary: Packaged documents are markdown reference topics embedded in the installed you binary and printed with you docs.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: A packaged document is a markdown reference topic shipped inside the installed you binary, referenced by topic id such as agents, config, or packaged-goal. Print the packaged topic index first, then pass a supported topic id to print that topic’s authored markdown. No live factory is required. Factory-local docs under a factory root are separate from these binary-embedded topics. Open the CLI documentation page when you need the broader command matrix beyond packaged topic lookup.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: Packaged documents covers packaged you docs topics. It is not a full topic dump, not a sync of packaged markdown into the site, not factory-local factory/docs authorship, and not the CLI install or command-matrix deep page. Sibling documentation links aid discovery only.

links.indexCommandLabel: Print the quick-start blurb and packaged topic index:
links.topicCommandLabel: Print one authored topic (agents is the orientation topic):

discovery.body: Discovery starts from you docs with no topic: that command prints the quick-start blurb and the packaged topic index. A supported topic argument prints that topic’s authored markdown with no wrapper formatting.

referenceAndFreshness.body: Reference topics by topic id from the packaged index. Content freshness tracks the installed CLI binary—upgrade the CLI to receive updated packaged topics. The web docs are not a live sync of packaged markdown.

exampleNotes.body: Replace agents with any other supported topic id from the index, such as config or packaged-goal, when you need that topic’s authored markdown.
